version.description: IR schema version this document targets (for example `0.5.0`). Validation rejects any value not listed in [`SUPPORTED_VERSIONS`].

package.description: Package identity used to name, version, and describe every generated consumer package (npm, PyPI, gem, NuGet, pub.dev, SwiftPM, Gradle, Go). When omitted, generators fall back to the IDL file stem and version `0.1.0`, but publishable artifacts should always set this explicitly.

modules.description: Top-level modules that make up the API surface. Each is an independent namespace; modules may nest further through [`Module::modules`].

generators.description: Per-generator configuration keyed by backend name (for example `swift` or `python`). The opaque [`toml::Value`] payload is interpreted by each generator, so unrecognized keys pass through untouched. `None` when the IDL declares no `generators:` block.

Package.description: Package identity for the generated consumer artifacts.

name.description: Canonical package name (e.g. `kvstore`). Per-target name overrides in `generators:` (such as `python.package_name`) still take precedence.

version.description: Semantic version stamped into every manifest (e.g. `1.2.0`).

description.description: Short summary written into each manifest's description field. Omitted from generated manifests when absent.

license.description: License identifier, typically an SPDX expression such as `MIT` or `Apache-2.0`, written into each manifest's license field.

authors.description: Package authors, each commonly formatted as `Name <email>`, mapped to whatever author or maintainer field the target ecosystem uses. Empty by default.

homepage.description: Project homepage URL recorded in manifests that expose one.

repository.description: Source repository URL recorded in manifests that expose one.

Module.description: A WeaveFFI module: a named group of functions, types, callbacks, listeners, and errors.

name.description: Module name, used as a namespace segment and a symbol-prefix component in generated code (for example `contacts`).

functions.description: Free functions this module exports across the FFI boundary.

interfaces.description: Interface (object) types declared in this module: stateful resources with constructors, methods, and static functions.

structs.description: Record (struct) types declared in this module.

enums.description: Enum types, C-style or algebraic, declared in this module.

callbacks.description: Callback signatures this module's functions and listeners can invoke.

listeners.description: Event listeners (subscribe and unsubscribe endpoints) this module exposes.

errors.description: Optional error domain: the named codes this module's fallible functions report. `None` when the module declares no errors.

modules.description: Nested submodules, forming a tree that mirrors a package hierarchy.

Function.description:
A function exported across the FFI boundary.

Each function becomes a C ABI entry point plus an idiomatic wrapper in every target language. The `async` and `cancellable` flags change how the symbol is lowered (a completion callback, an extra cancel-token parameter) without altering the parameter and return shape declared here. The same shape also describes an interface's constructors, methods, and statics (see [`InterfaceDef`]).

name.description: Function name, lowered to a per-language symbol (for example `create_contact`).

params.description: Ordered parameter list; order is preserved in every generated signature.

return.description: Return type, or `None` for a function that returns nothing. Serialized under the IDL key `return`.

doc.description: Human-readable documentation, propagated to the generated bindings' doc comments. `None` when undocumented.

throws.description: Whether the function can fail with a domain error. A throwing function surfaces as `throws`/`raises` in the idiomatic bindings, reporting the owning module's error domain; a non-throwing function has a plain signature and treats any error as a producer bug (a trap, not a typed error). Defaults to `false`.

async.description: Whether the function is asynchronous, lowering to a completion-callback form rather than a blocking call. Serialized under the IDL key `async`.

cancellable.description: Whether an async call accepts a cancellation token so callers can request that an in-flight operation stop early. Defaults to `false`.

deprecated.description: Deprecation notice; when set, generators emit a deprecation annotation carrying this message. `None` means the function is current.

since.description: Version in which the function was introduced (for example `0.2.0`), surfaced as a "since" annotation where the target language supports one.

Param.description: A single parameter of a [`Function`] or [`CallbackDef`].

name.description: Parameter name as it appears in generated signatures (for example `id`).

type.description: Parameter type. Serialized under the IDL key `type`.

mutable.description: Whether the callee may write back through this parameter (for example a buffer filled in place). Defaults to `false`.

doc.description: Human-readable documentation for the parameter, propagated to the generated bindings. `None` when undocumented.

TypeRef.title: TypeRef
TypeRef.description: Reference to a type. Encoded as a string with custom syntax: primitives (`i8`, `i16`, `i32`, `i64`, `u8`, `u16`, `u32`, `u64`, `f32`, `f64`, `bool`, `string`, `bytes`, `handle`), borrowed types (`&str`, `&[u8]`), typed handles (`handle<{name}>`), iterators (`iter<{T}>`), lists (`[{T}]`), maps (`{{K:V}}`), optionals (`{T}?`), or any user-defined struct/enum/interface name.

InterfaceDef.description: An interface: an opaque object type with constructors, methods, and statics.

doc.description: Human-readable documentation, propagated to the generated bindings. `None` when undocumented.

constructors.description: Constructors: static functions returning a new instance. A constructor declares no `return` (the instance is implicit) and may not be `async`.

methods.description: Instance methods. Each lowers with an implicit leading `self` slot (a pointer to the interface object) before the declared parameters.

statics.description: Static functions namespaced under the interface but taking no `self`.

StructDef.description: A struct (record) type with named fields.

doc.description: Human-readable documentation, propagated to the generated bindings. `None` when undocumented.

fields.description: The fields in declaration order; order is preserved in the generated type and its constructors.

builder.description: Whether to also emit a builder API for constructing the struct field by field, alongside the all-fields constructor. Defaults to `false`.

StructField.description: A named field of a [`StructDef`], or the payload of an algebraic [`EnumVariant`].

type.description: Field type. Serialized under the IDL key `type`.

doc.description: Human-readable documentation, propagated to the generated bindings. `None` when undocumented.

default.description: Default value used when the field is omitted, kept as a raw YAML value so any literal the field's type accepts can be expressed. Ignored for algebraic [`EnumVariant`] payloads, which aren't defaultable. `None` when the field has no default.

EnumDef.description: An enum type. C-style when every variant is a bare discriminant; an algebraic sum type when any variant declares fields.

doc.description: Human-readable documentation, propagated to the generated bindings. `None` when undocumented.

variants.description: The variants in declaration order. Whether any of them carries fields decides if this is a C-style or an algebraic enum.

value.description: Integer discriminant. Doubles as the C-style enum value and as the runtime tag that distinguishes the variants of an algebraic enum.

doc.description: Human-readable documentation, propagated to the generated bindings. `None` when undocumented.

fields.description: Associated data carried by this variant. Empty for a unit variant or a C-style enum; non-empty makes the owning enum a sum type (see [`EnumDef::is_rich`]). Variant fields reuse [`StructField`] but ignore the `default` slot (a sum-type payload has no defaultable fields).

CallbackDef.description:
A callback signature: a function shape the host implements and native code invokes.

Callbacks are declared at module scope rather than as a [`TypeRef`] so the C ABI can represent them uniformly as a function pointer plus a context pointer. A [`ListenerDef`] references one by name to model an event stream.

name.description: Callback name, used to name the generated function-pointer type and referenced by [`ListenerDef::event_callback`] (for example `on_message`).

params.description: Parameters passed to the callback each time it fires.

doc.description: Human-readable documentation, propagated to the generated bindings. `None` when undocumented.

ListenerDef.description:
An event listener: a subscribe and unsubscribe endpoint that delivers events through a [`CallbackDef`].

Generators expand a listener into register and unregister functions; the register call takes the named callback and returns a subscription id the caller later hands to unregister.

name.description: Listener name, lowered into the generated `register_*` and `unregister_*` function names (for example `messages`).

event_callback.description: Name of the [`CallbackDef`] invoked for each event. Must match a callback declared on the same [`Module`].

doc.description: Human-readable documentation, propagated to the generated bindings. `None` when undocumented.

ErrorDomain.description: A module's error domain: the named set of error codes its fallible functions can report.

name.description: Error domain name, used to name the generated error type (for example `ContactErrors`).

codes.description: The error codes that belong to this domain.

ErrorCode.description: A single named error within an [`ErrorDomain`].

name.description: Error code name, lowered to a variant or constant on the generated error type (for example `not_found`).

code.description: Stable numeric value carried across the C ABI to identify this error.

message.description: Default human-readable message describing the error.

doc.description: Human-readable documentation, propagated to the generated bindings. `None` when undocumented.
